title: I Can't Believe It's Not Filter
author: Rohan
notes:
Make actual filter coffee on an espresso machine — coarse grind, slow controlled flow, hands-off. By Rohan (pocketsciencecoffee.com).

Approach: drip water onto the puck at about the rate it drips from a filter bed — target ~1 ml/s at 96°C through a coarsely-ground bed (~600µm from chirp; ~500µm if you don't have a hog/porcupress tool). Dose 2–3g under the basket's rating (e.g. 15g in an 18g basket), non-lab filter paper at the bottom, WDT flat, pierce with a porcupress-style tool, don't tamp — just level with a puck screen. Yields ~15× the dose (≈1:17 with retained water), TDS ~1.3–1.6. A bloom is available but usually left off; optional declining temperature (3°C per minute after the first minute) helps lesser coffees. Dial with ratio.

On the name: "I came up with this profile in my attempt to make a sprover but in the process realized that it's quite possible to make consistently delicious filter coffee with decent amount of flavor separation while yielding reasonably and do so in a hands-off manner. … The taste has been consistently good enough for me to exclaim out loud and hence name the profile 'I Can't Believe It's Not Filter'."

Full write-up: https://pocketsciencecoffee.com/2023/09/10/making-filter-coffee-with-an-espresso-machine/
